danger_signals.visual_form: Bright orange/yellow cone markers positioned along race route edges
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates nearby weapon pickup location; signals hazard or combat opportunity ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Skater animation change to aggressive posture, weapon raised
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is about to attack; player should evade or counter-attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation of skates/protective gear in equipment menu; cracked/damaged appearance
danger_signals.meaning: High risk of blowout or crash injury if not replaced before next race

danger_signals.visual_form: Police car model distinct from civilian traffic (likely marked/colored differently)
danger_signals.meaning: Skitching on police bumper carries apprehension/fine risk; higher reward but higher danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Skater ragdoll physics, sprawl animation on pavement
danger_signals.meaning: Player has taken damage; hospital visit possible with worn gear; financial penalty incoming

safety_signals.visual_form: Civilian car models on street; standard traffic flow
safety_signals.meaning: Safe skitching target; low-risk momentum source; standard reward

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, unobstructed track path with minimal traffic congestion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe acceleration zone; reduced collision/combat risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible ramp structures along race route
safety_signals.meaning: Safe opportunity to perform tricks for judges' rating and money; low combat risk during aerial maneuver

safety_signals.visual_form: Clean, undamaged skate/gear appearance in equipment menu
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to race; no blowout or injury penalty risk

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Inline skater silhouette; skateboard culture aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban street racing circuits across 12 cities (Vancouver, New York, San Diego, etc.); graffiti art integration suggests youth culture spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied character models; rotoscoped skater animations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Some wield weapons (nunchaku, chains, pipes, crowbars, whips, baseball bats); increasing aggression as tournament progresses
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Share same urban streets as player; participate in same tournament bracket system

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police vehicle model (distinct from civilian traffic)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Police cars are skitchable but carry enforcement risk
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present on race courses as hazard/opportunity; represents legal authority within urban racing world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Graffiti art (created by real Toronto graffiti artists Carlos Zelaya, Zack Benwell, Nelson Garcia)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Graffiti adorns urban environments; indicates youth/street subculture dominance of racing spaces; grunge aesthetic in soundtrack

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slower civilian vehicles; fewer weapons on course; opponent skaters less visually aggressive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents are less aggressive; cars are easier to skitch; lower difficulty overall

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Faster vehicles; more weapon pickups visible; opponent skaters with more active combat postures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents become noticeably more aggressive; cars become faster and harder to skitch; increased combat intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipment condition in shop menu; gear wear state visualized
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Poor players must manage entry fees and equipment replacement; rich players can afford premium gear and take risks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Password system; race entry fee UI; tournament bracket progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Successfully completing races unlocks new cities; failure to finish top 5 prevents progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skates/protective gear show visual wear in equipment menu; cracking/damage appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Worn skates cause blowout during race; worn protective gear causes costly hospital visits; forces resource management

environmental_storytelling.example: Real Toronto graffiti photographed and incorporated into urban racing environments; creates authentic street culture authenticity and suggests player operates in youth subculture spaces
Tournament spans 12 real North American cities (Vancouver, NYC, San Diego, etc.); geography progression suggests expanding notoriety and increasingly competitive racing circuit
Civilian cars vs. police cars vs. increasingly fast vehicles; visual distinction signals mechanical challenge escalation and rule-breaking risk elevation
Traffic cones indicate nearby weapons; visual roadside markers guide player attention to combat opportunities and environmental hazards
Damaged/cracked skate appearance in menu; visual representation of resource attrition and financial pressure accumulation
Judges materialize in lower-left corner when tricks are performed; signals cultural acknowledgment of skater artistry within racing context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can race without blowout risk; can sustain crashes without hospital penalty; full combat capability
High blowout risk during race; hospital visit penalty on crash; limited ability to sustain combat
Lower aggression; minimal combat initiation; basic attack patterns
High aggression; frequent combat initiation; weapon wielding; active attempt to shove skaters off vehicles during skitching
Provide skitching opportunity; standard momentum boost; no apprehension risk
Provide skitching opportunity with apprehension risk; higher monetary reward; risk of fines/apprehension if police contact occurs
Actively strike with visible weapon; weapons can be disarmed by attacking opponent; player can steal and use taken weapons

notes: Skitchin' communicates world-state primarily through equipment degradation mechanics and opponent behavior scaling. The game uses visual cues (traffic cones, weapon animations, vehicle types) to signal immediate tactical information rather than broad world conditions. The urban graffiti aesthetic and multi-city progression create an implicit narrative of street subculture expansion. Police presence adds asymmetric risk/reward dynamic. Tournament structure with password saves suggests incremental world progression where difficulty intensifies visually and behaviorally with each city advancement. The game emphasizes youth street culture identity through graffiti integration and grunge soundtrack, positioning the player as participant in counter-cultural racing scene.
